rationale: Bowman et al. is discussed in multiple substantive sections (background, contribution framing, and results interpretation), not just listed as general prior work. The citing paper summarizes specific Bowman et al. findings, then explicitly points out limitations in that study's advertising measurement and identification strategy. It positions its own design as addressing those limitations and quantifying economic magnitude, and later compares its estimates to Bowman et al. on channel and advertising-response patterns.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Bowman et al. (2004) as a key prior study of compliance and advertising heterogeneity, but tightens the analysis by using claims plus expenditure data with stronger controls for brand effects and demand shocks. It refines the prior evidence by separating own versus other-brand effects and offering economic-magnitude estimates while qualifying when advertising can reduce compliance.
